skill.name: device-management
skill.description: Manage UniFi device adoption, inventory, controller status, and configuration review across network sites.
skill.summary: Track UniFi devices from adoption through inventory, monitoring, and hardware planning.
skill.author: DataKnifeAI

security_audit.summary: Static findings for weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and most external commands are false positives from Markdown text and tool names. The confirmed risk is a documentation example that uses curl -k with an API key header, which can encourage disabled TLS verification and sensitive header exposure.

medium_findings.title: Insecure API Example Disables TLS Verification
medium_findings.description: The documentation example uses curl -k while sending X-API-KEY from an environment variable. This is not executed by the skill, but it encourages users to disable certificate validation when calling a UniFi API endpoint.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The curl example is explicit and includes both -k and an API key header. Risk is limited because this is documentation, not executable code.

low_findings.title: Documentation-Only Command Tokens
low_findings.description: Most external command detections are Markdown backticks around tool names, site ID examples, or workflow steps. They do not execute shell commands or pass user input to a command runner.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are prose and Markdown formatting, not scripts. No executable handler, shell expansion, or command invocation path is present in those locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are skill description text and device workflow text. No weak cryptographic algorithm, scanner command, or reconnaissance logic appears in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found natural-language phrases only. There is no code or instruction for cryptographic operations or network scanning.

dangerous_patterns.title: API Key Header in Curl Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill shows an API call pattern that reads UNIFI_API_KEY and sends it in an X-API-KEY header. Users should avoid logging commands and should prefer verified TLS connections.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The sensitive header usage is clear in the documentation. The finding is moderate because the variable is referenced, not hardcoded or transmitted by skill code.

content.value_statement: UniFi teams need clear device adoption status, inventory records, and controller health context. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through device review workflows using the available UniFi MCP tools.
actual_capabilities: Lists UniFi devices that are pending adoption.
Reviews adopted network devices and inventory details.
Retrieves device specifications, firmware versions, and status data.
Checks UniFi controller information and system status.
Organizes device information by type, location, and operational role.
Supports upgrade and replacement planning from available device data.
limitations: Requires configured UniFi MCP tools and valid controller access.
Does not directly adopt devices unless the connected tools support that action.
Cannot verify physical device location without accurate inventory data.
Recommendations depend on the completeness of controller and device telemetry.

use_cases.title: Prepare New Device Adoption
use_cases.description: Review pending UniFi devices, identify their model and address details, and plan where each device should be onboarded.

use_cases.title: Maintain Device Inventory
use_cases.description: Create a current inventory grouped by device type, site, location, firmware version, and operational purpose.

use_cases.title: Plan Hardware Refreshes
use_cases.description: Use model, firmware, and status information to identify devices that need upgrades, replacements, or maintenance windows.

prompt_templates.title: Show Pending Devices
prompt_templates.prompt: Show all UniFi devices pending adoption. Include MAC address, IP address, model, and any site information available.

prompt_templates.title: Build Inventory Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a UniFi device inventory summary grouped by device type and location. Highlight missing names, old firmware, and unclear ownership.

prompt_templates.title: Review Controller Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the UniFi controller status and summarize version, uptime, system health, and any device status issues that need attention.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Upgrade Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the current UniFi device inventory and create an upgrade strategy by age, model, firmware, performance risk, and maintenance priority.

output_examples.output: A concise adoption queue with each pending device, its MAC address, IP address, model, likely role, and recommended next action.

output_examples.input: Create a device inventory report by type.
output: A grouped inventory covering switches, gateways, access points, and other UniFi devices.
Each group includes model, firmware, status, location notes, and gaps that should be corrected.

output_examples.output: A prioritized refresh plan that identifies aging hardware, firmware concerns, replacement candidates, and suggested maintenance windows.

best_practices: Use verified controller credentials and avoid exposing API keys in shared logs.
Adopt devices in planned groups by site, function, and maintenance window.
Keep device names, locations, firmware versions, and ownership records current.
anti_patterns: Do not use insecure TLS settings for routine controller access.
Do not adopt unknown devices without confirming physical location and ownership.
Do not treat generated upgrade recommendations as a substitute for capacity planning.

faq.question: What tools does this skill expect?
faq.answer: It expects UniFi MCP tools for pending devices, network devices, device stats, and network information.

faq.question: Can it adopt a device automatically?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on review and planning. Actual adoption depends on the connected MCP server tools.

faq.question: Does it store API keys?
faq.answer: No evidence found that this skill stores API keys. It references an environment variable in documentation.

faq.question: Why can a site ID be empty?
faq.answer: The skill notes that an empty site ID can represent the default UniFi site in API responses.

faq.question: Can it help with firmware planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use available model, firmware, and status data to support upgrade planning.

faq.question: Is this safe for production use?
faq.answer: Use it with configured UniFi tools and verified TLS. The audit found documentation risk, not executable malware.
